18.reason: [Error: The input format specifies that operations and values are separated by spaces, but not every operation has a second integer. For example, operation 0 (pop) and operation -1 (end) are single integers. This scanf expects two integers per line, which will cause incorrect reading or undefined behavior when only one integer is provided.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: The input format specifies that operations and values are separated by spaces, but not every operation has a second integer. For example, operation 0 (pop) and operation -1 (end) are single integers. This scanf expects two integers per line, which will cause incorrect reading or undefined behavior when only one integer is provided.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  Error: The input format is a sequence of integers separated by spaces, not always pairs. When n is 0 or -1, there is no second integer m to read. This will cause incorrect input parsing and may read the next operation as m.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: This reads two integers at once, but for pop operation (n=0) no second integer is provided. This causes the program to misread subsequent operations. Should read n first, then if n==1 read m separately.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  This scanf expects two integers per iteration, but the input format is a sequence of integers where push operations are two numbers and pop/end are single numbers. This will cause misreading of subsequent operations, e.g., a pop operation (0) will also consume the next number (which may be a value for a push or another operation).
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  Reads two integers unconditionally, but m should only be read when n == 1. For pop (0) and end (-1) operations, there is no second integer, causing input misalignment and incorrect behavior.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The code unconditionally reads two integers, but the second integer (value to push) is only provided when n==1. For pop (0) or end (-1) operations, reading a second integer consumes the next operation token, leading to incorrect parsing. It should read n first, and only read m inside the if(n==1) block.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The program always reads two integers, but for pop (n=0) and end (n=-1), no second integer should be read. This causes incorrect input consumption and behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The program always reads two integers, but for pop (n=0) and end (n=-1), no second integer should be read. This causes incorrect input consumption and logic errors.]
